blocker: IC-02 remains unresolved: prd.md:235 still requires every worker-marked child to be excluded from title scheduling, contradicting prd.md:198,227,231,233,383 and 549, which make ordinary registered conversational children eligible after the 150-second debounce.

evidence: prd.md:197 explicitly replaces the former wide-only NOW placement and primary-row context gauge with one all-layout subordinate row.
prd.md:344 removes wide primary-row and narrow/stack inline context telemetry.
prd.md:371-378 defines exactly one context-first detail row after identity and before the sub-agent strip, including live, missing, stale, and dead truth-table cases.
prd.md:389 pins wide@168, wide@120, narrow@100, stack@60, CJK-safe clipping, row ordering, and terminal-width bounds.
prd.md:343 retires the title-only PID join and assigns title, NOW, and context to one association.

prd.md:379 requires exact (pid,proc_start), permits only one same-harness realpath(cwd) fallback when exact identity is absent, rejects PID reuse, duplicate PID, cwd ambiguity, and cross-harness matching, and forbids parent-context inheritance.
prd.md:390 pins joint adoption and refusal fixtures for PID reuse, duplicate PID, two cwd candidates, cross-harness candidates, and differing parent/child context.

prd.md:303 now makes the active F-28 consumer clause fail closed whenever any explicit route tuple member exists but record parsing, hash, or identity validation fails; pipe/artifact fallback is limited to complete tuple absence.
prd.md:320 repeats that boundary and limits registry-exact evidence without a verified record to observed identity fields, excluding topology, progress, gate passage, and node completion claims.
prd.md:361-369 defines the same exact/registry/artifact authority order, stable ambiguity codes, and prohibition on artifact fallthrough after explicit conflicts.
prd.md:572 updates the locked F-28 decision to the same explicit-tuple boundary.
prd.md:369 allows artifact inference only with no route tuple and one exact plan-directory candidate, permits stage_label only, and forbids route, node, progress, gate, and completion synthesis.
prd.md:392 pins invalid explicit tuple plus plausible artifact as unknown/ambiguous rather than artifact-derived.
prd.md:393 separately covers one, zero, and multiple artifact candidates and requires multiple-artifact ambiguity evidence.
prd.md:228-231 and 383 make F-39 the numeric authority for default concurrency 3, hard maximum 4, four starts per rolling 60 seconds, and 600/150-second main/child debounces.

prd.md:383 requires the shared cross-process pool, per-session locks, stale-lease recovery, both kill switches, default-provider tool denial, shell-free custom argv, and wrapper-owned custom no-tools enforcement.
prd.md:548-549 explicitly supersedes the old title size and revises the historical F-23 lock to the current quota values.
prd.md:398-399 pins hermetic quota and zero-provider-call acceptance.
prd.md:381 makes missing, stale, malformed, or source-sequence-regressed telemetry unknown and explicitly allows a numeric decrease caused by compaction.
prd.md:397 repeats that numeric decrease alone is valid while source-sequence regression becomes unknown.
pipeline_state.yaml:11-17 records spec done, corrected v16 development pending, and resume scope F-36 through F-39.
pipeline_summary.md:20-23 records the pre-correction v16 snapshot, correction-only transaction, specification-only scope, and dev pending state.

The assigned pre-correction snapshot has SHA-256 d7e3ffbdef592ef359066b87d90b8e31740e0412616bb822822e251a59a231ad; the current PRD has SHA-256 791b983bf96906903bbe22adef4db8e63ef80f47d6d6e6cdc11571653b793cf7.

A full unified diff between the pre-correction snapshot and current PRD contains only the declared F-17/F-22 title correction, F-24 eligibility correction, F-28 fail-closed correction, historical F-22 supersession, and replacement of the old Next block; unchanged regions are byte-identical.

The immutable earlier verdicts retained SHA-256 430a8d12314b94bc326e57210cb8ed943b225a438945bb2d080482a6b0fbc4a5 and 3df681752d260db67d27c8266384fb434c63b16a0caf40170d29765fbc3ea0a4 during this review.
prd.md:360 assigns source-agnostic resolution to projection.py and prohibits render, --once, and --json from independently re-running live_stage().
prd.md module structure names projection.py as the Session/DispatchJob evidence to WorkProjection resolver.
prd.md component diagram routes Session/DispatchJob evidence through WorkProjection before group/process/plain rendering and additive JSON.

ic_dispositions.evidence: prd.md:303,320,361-369 and 572 consistently permit pipe/artifact fallback only when the full route tuple is absent and keep invalid or conflicting explicit tuples unknown/ambiguous.

prd.md:233 correctly makes AGENT_SESSION_ROLE=worker attribution rather than exclusion and allows ordinary registered conversational children after 150 seconds, but prd.md:235 still requires a worker-marked child to be excluded from the title scheduler. This directly contradicts prd.md:198,227,231,383 and 549 and leaves two incompatible acceptance outcomes.
prd.md:195,197,222,548 and 594 agree on one current TITLE validation contract of 3-6 words and at most 40 characters; v6/v9 sizes are explicitly historical or superseded.

The current PRD has exactly one Next heading at prd.md:590; prd.md:592-595 contains only F-36 through F-39 implementation and hermetic acceptance work, and prd.md:597 explicitly classifies the prior v2/v6/v8/v9/v10 sequences as completed or superseded history.

internal_consistency.blocking_issue: The F-24 acceptance sentence at prd.md:235 was not updated with the F-24 eligibility clause at prd.md:233, so the same ordinary registered child is both eligible and ineligible for title/NOW scheduling.
internal_consistency.assessment: IC-01, IC-03, and IC-04 are closed, and the remaining v16 authority, projection, layout, context, quota, and handoff clauses are mutually coherent. IC-02 is still a live normative contradiction rather than historical text, so the full PRD is not internally consistent.
internal_consistency.required_resolution: Change the F-24 acceptance sentence to assert attribution plus eligibility under the single rule: ordinary registered children with conversational transcripts and no explicit internal/non-conversational exclusion tag are scheduled after the 150-second child debounce; mem/title workers, app-server, dead/stale rows, and transcript-less internal loop/cron rows remain excluded.

implementation_readiness.assessment: The v16 design is otherwise implementation-ready: ownership, additive schemas, evidence precedence, ambiguity codes, arbitrary DAG behavior, layout truth tables, quota constants, hermetic checks, mirror parity, and public JSON compatibility are explicit. Autopilot-code should not start while IC-02 permits opposite scheduler behavior and opposite acceptance assertions for the same registered child fixture.
ready_elements: One WorkProjection resolver and all consumer surfaces are named.
Exact, registry-only, artifact-only, and ambiguity boundaries are testable.
Arbitrary composed DAG, parallel sibling, fan-in, width/overflow, additive JSON, and context orthogonality fixtures are complete.
The numeric quota, debounce, fake-clock, isolated-state-root, mirror, public-JSON, and zero-live-provider gates are explicit.
The pre-correction snapshot and both earlier verdicts remain unchanged, and metadata truthfully records spec corrected/done with development pending.
implementation_readiness.completion_gate: Reconcile prd.md:235 with the single F-24/F-39 child eligibility predicate, then rerun an independent final review before starting the v16 autopilot-code handoff.

all_layout_width_overflow_cjk.evidence: prd.md:224 and 389 cover wide@168, wide@120, narrow@100, stack@60, terminal-width bounds, and display-cell/CJK-safe clipping.

single_context_now_row.evidence: prd.md:197,344 and 371-378 define exactly one context-first subordinate row in every layout and the complete live/missing/stale/dead truth table.

joint_child_association_and_refusal.evidence: prd.md:343,379 and 390 jointly bind title/NOW/context and refuse PID reuse, duplicate PID, multiple cwd, and cross-harness candidates without parent-context inheritance.

exact_registry_artifact_authority.evidence: prd.md:303,320 and 361-369 define verified-record, registry-exact, unique-cwd, and route-absent artifact authority with fail-closed conflicts.

artifact_candidate_cardinality.evidence: prd.md:369,392-393 cover one, zero, and multiple artifact candidates, stage-label-only inference, and invalid-explicit-tuple refusal.

arbitrary_composed_dag_parallel_fan_in.evidence: prd.md:368 and 394 preserve opaque IDs, unit, gate, scope, depends_on topology, record-order levels, parallel active siblings, fan-in, progress, and absence of _PIPE_STAGES for sealed records.

additive_json_compatibility.evidence: prd.md:360,379 and 396 preserve sessions, jobs, summary, route, and prior child-field meanings while adding work_projection and child context; old-key-only consumers must pass.

session_dispatch_projection_parity.evidence: prd.md:358-360 and 391 require equivalent exact Session and DispatchJob evidence to yield the same normalized route, node, state, and progress across group/process/plain/JSON.

context_intensity_orthogonality.evidence: prd.md:381 and 397 make 69/70/85 affect band/render only, preserve graph/depth/intensity/model/effort/QA/test/gate/guard, allow numeric decreases, and reject source-sequence regression.

title_quota_debounce_and_eligibility.evidence: prd.md:228,231,383 and 398 completely specify default concurrency 3, hard 4, four starts per rolling 60 seconds, 600/150-second debounces, shared leases, fake clock, isolated state root, 200-session backlog, fifth-start refusal, window recovery, and direct-runner enforcement; however prd.md:235 contradicts the ordinary-child eligibility predicate used by those tests.

mirror_and_public_json.evidence: prd.md:400 and 595 require canonical-to-Claude mirror byte/semantic parity and public JSON compatibility as implementation gates.

zero_live_provider_invocation.evidence: prd.md:383,399 and 595 prohibit provider startup in JSON/once/demo/test paths and require fail-if-reached guards around default, custom, and live provider process entry points. No live provider was invoked by this review.

snapshot_and_pipeline_state.evidence: The pre-correction v16 snapshot is preserved at SHA-256 d7e3ffbdef592ef359066b87d90b8e31740e0412616bb822822e251a59a231ad; pipeline_state.yaml:11-17 records spec done/corrected and dev pending for F-36 through F-39.

acceptance_boundaries.note: These are specification-coverage results. The selected 179 passing tests validate the current baseline and transaction tooling, not the unimplemented v16 WorkProjection, layout, association, or revised quota behavior.

qa_policy_evidence.independence: This fresh sealed review-final-2 route is the independent deep-review pass. The bounded review worker did not dispatch another worker.
qa_policy_evidence.baseline_result: 179/179 selected read-only baseline tests passed: Fleet 167, compose-route 9, spec-transaction 3. They validate current baseline behavior and tooling only, not unimplemented v16 acceptance.

unsupported_runtime_contracts: The spec-read marker side effect could not write under /home/Uihyeop/agent_setting/.spec-grounding because that worker-visible location is read-only; the PRD itself was read completely.

No live default or custom title provider was invoked. Provider quotas, costs, and external behavior were not empirically measured; only specification boundaries and hermetic repository tests were reviewed.
A custom FLEET_TITLE_COMMAND wrapper's no-tools property cannot be enforced or introspected by Fleet beyond the shell-free argv contract; that property remains the wrapper owner's responsibility.

The selected baseline suite predates v16 implementation and cannot prove WorkProjection, the new all-layout detail row, unified child association, revised 3/4 quota behavior, or zero-call implementation until the subsequent autopilot-code cycle adds the specified fixtures.

The required post-write file-write heartbeat was attempted after the baseline test heartbeat, but the registry rejected the phase transition as progress-phase-regression (test->file-write). The durable artifact is finalized below with the allowed terminal artifact phase.
